I guess this is not a must. LLVM is clever enough to eliminate the error path:
```julia
julia> function myrem2pi(x::Integer, r::RoundingMode)                                          
           fx = float(x)                                                                       
               fx == x || throw(ArgumentError(LazyString(typeof(x), " argument to rem2pi is too large: ", x)))                                                                                
           rem2pi(fx, r)                                                                       
       end
myrem2pi (generic function with 1 method)

julia> @code_llvm myrem2pi(Int32(1), RoundUp)
; Function Signature: myrem2pi(Int32, Base.Rounding.RoundingMode{:Up})
;  @ REPL[1]:1 within `myrem2pi`
; Function Attrs: uwtable
define double @julia_myrem2pi_6707(i32 signext %"x::Int32") #0 {
L5:
;  @ REPL[1]:2 within `myrem2pi`
; ┌ @ float.jl:356 within `float`
; │┌ @ float.jl:329 within `AbstractFloat`
; ││┌ @ float.jl:221 within `Float64`
     %0 = sitofp i32 %"x::Int32" to double
; └└└
;  @ REPL[1]:4 within `myrem2pi`
  %1 = call double @j_rem2pi_6713(double %0)
  ret double %1
}
```

functions"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-03-23T18:04:42Z","updated_at":"2024-01-21T11:16:14Z","closed_at":"2024-01-21T11:16:14Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Currently we only defined `rem2pi(x::Int32, r::RoundingMode)`, `rem2pi(x::Int64, r::RoundingMode)`.
A general fallback seems make sense.
```julia
function rem2pi(x::Integer, r::RoundingMode)
    fx = float(x)
    fx == x || throw(ArgumentError("$(typeof(x)) argument to rem2pi is too large: $x"))
    rem2pi(fx, r)
end
```

{"action":"closed","number":52626,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/52626","id":1656135841,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5itqCh","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52626","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52626.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52626.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52626","number":52626,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Avoid fallback to scalar indexing in slicing wrapped gpu arrays","user":{"login":"nomadbl","id":17994440,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE3OTk0NDQw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/17994440?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl","html_url":"https://github.com/nomadbl","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadbl/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"On GPU, wrapper types such as Transpose and Adjoint are missing handling of `::Colon` on `getindex`, leading to fallback to so called scalar indexing which is much slower on GPU (causes memory copying from device to host).

MWE without this fix:
```julia
julia> using LinearAlgebra, JLArrays
julia> JLArrays.allowscalar(false)
julia> x = rand(Float32, 2,2) |> jl ; y = transpose(x);
julia> y[:,1]
ERROR: Scalar indexing is disallowed.
Invocation of getindex resulted in scalar indexing of a GPU array.
This is typically caused by calling an iterating implementation of a method.
Such implementations *do not* execute on the GPU, but very slowly on the CPU,
and therefore should be avoided.

If you want to allow scalar iteration, use `allowscalar` or `@allowscalar`
to enable scalar iteration globally or for the operations in question.
Stacktrace:
  [1] error(s::String)
    @ Base ./error.jl:35
  [2] errorscalar(op::String)
    @ GPUArraysCore ~/.julia/packages/GPUArraysCore/GMsgk/src/GPUArraysCore.jl:155

    @ GPUArraysCore ~/.julia/packages/GPUArraysCore/GMsgk/src/GPUArraysCore.jl:128

    @ GPUArraysCore ~/.julia/packages/GPUArraysCore/GMsgk/src/GPUArraysCore.jl:116
  [5] getindex
    @ ~/.julia/packages/GPUArrays/EoKy0/src/host/indexing.jl:48 [inlined]
  [6] scalar_getindex
    @ ~/.julia/packages/GPUArrays/EoKy0/src/host/indexing.jl:34 [inlined]
  [7] _getindex
    @ ~/.julia/packages/GPUArrays/EoKy0/src/host/indexing.jl:17 [inlined]
  [8] getindex
    @ ~/.julia/packages/GPUArrays/EoKy0/src/host/indexing.jl:15 [inlined]
  [9] getindex
    @ ~/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/adjtrans.jl:334 [inlined]

    @ ./multidimensional.jl:921 [inlined]
 [11] macro expansion
    @ ./cartesian.jl:64 [inlined]

    @ ./multidimensional.jl:916 [inlined]

    @ Base ./multidimensional.jl:907
 [14] _getindex
    @ Base ./multidimensional.jl:893 [inlined]
 [15] getindex(::Transpose{Float32, JLArray{Float32, 2}}, ::Function, ::Int64)
    @ Base ./abstractarray.jl:1314
 [16] top-level scope
    @ REPL[10]:1
```

After adding the missing path with `::Colon`:
```julia
julia> using LinearAlgebra, JLArrays
julia> JLArrays.allowscalar(false)
julia> x = rand(Float32, 2,2) |> jl ; y = transpose(x);
julia> display(y[:,1])
2-element JLArray{Float32, 1}:
 0.5699245
 0.44491202
julia> y[:,1] == x[1,:]
true
```

Any thoughts and comments are appreciated, as well as directing me to where the equivalent code should be in the case of `PermutedDimsArray` and any other similar cases of {"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":16759927243,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"32ad80bbdb6d80589e0232221dd66e52a8da205b","before":"e6bf976fdf634644e664eaeee84d94cffaf33b91","commits":[{"sha":"32ad80bbdb6d80589e0232221dd66e52a8da205b","author":{"email":"9724b4e6cd549fe9b1c21996f3f0108658b44517@gmail.com","name":"Jishnu Bhattacharya"},"message":"Add type assertion in iterate for logicalindex (#53015)

The type-assertion helps convey the information that
`iterate(tail(s)...)` would never return `nothing`, which makes JET
happy.
On master
```julia
julia> L = Base.LogicalIndex([true])
1-element Base.LogicalIndex{Int64, Vector{Bool}}:
 1

julia> @report_call iterate(L)
═════ 2 possible errors found ═════
┌ iterate(L::Base.LogicalIndex{Int64, Vector{Bool}}) @ Base ./multidimensional.jl:778
│┌ iterate(L::Base.LogicalIndex{Int64, Vector{Bool}}, s::Tuple{Int64, LinearIndices{1, Tuple{Base.OneTo{Int64}}}}) @ Base ./multidimensional.jl:789
││┌ indexed_iterate(I::Nothing, i::Int64) @ Base ./tuple.jl:94
│││ no matching method found `iterate(::Nothing)`: x = iterate(I::Nothing)
││└────────────────────
││┌ indexed_iterate(I::Nothing, i::Int64, state::Int64) @ Base ./tuple.jl:99
│││ no matching method found `iterate(::Nothing, ::Int64)`: x = iterate(I::Nothing, state::Int64)
```
This PR
```julia
julia> @report_call iterate(L)
No errors detected
```

Close https://github.com/JuliaArrays/StaticArrays.jl/issues/1225","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/32ad80bbdb6d80589e0232221dd66e52a8da205b"}]}

throwing"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-01-23T14:10:41Z","updated_at":"2024-01-24T12:26:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Here's a Git repository containing a Julia package that generates counterexamples to the transitivity property that `<:` is supposed to satisfy:

https://gitlab.com/nsajko/SubtypingRelationExperiments.jl

So far I tested it on v1.10 and v1.11-DEV: the results between these two versions are the same and may be found under `results/`. I'll give just two of the counterexamples here, see the rest on my repo:

This is the first counterexample in the results:

```
counterexample triplet:  (Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}, Tuple{C, C}, Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C)
```

In the REPL:

```julia-repl
julia> struct C end

julia> const T1 = Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}
Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}

julia> const T2 = Tuple{C, C}
Tuple{C, C}

julia> const T3 = Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C
Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C

julia> T1 <: T2 <: T3
true

julia> T1 <: T3
false
```

This is the last counterexample in the results:

```
counterexample triplet:  (Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C, Tuple{Any, Vararg{C}}, Tuple{Any, Vararg{X}} where X>:C)
```

In the REPL:

```julia-repl
julia> abstract type A end

julia> struct C <: A end

julia> const T1 = Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C
Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C

julia> const T2 = Tuple{Any, Vararg{C}}
Tuple{Any, Vararg{C}}

julia> const T3 = Tuple{Any, Vararg{X}} where X>:C
Tuple{Any, Vararg{X}} where X>:C

julia> T1 <: T2 <: T3
true

julia> T1 <: T3
false

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1466277090","pull_request_review_id":1843566421,"id":1466277090,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5XZZzi","diff_hunk":"@@ -556,6 +556,33 @@ static void isort_union(jl_value_t **a, size_t len) JL_NOTSAFEPOINT
     }
 }
 
+static int simple_subtype(jl_value_t *a, jl_value_t *b, int hasfree)
+{
+    if (a == jl_bottom_type || b == (jl_value_t*)jl_any_type)
+        return 1;
+    if (jl_egal(a, b))
+        return 1;
+    if (hasfree == 0) {
+        // issue #24521: don't merge Type{T} where typeof(T) varies
+        return !(jl_is_type_type(a) && jl_is_type_type(b) &&
+             jl_typeof(jl_tparam0(a)) != jl_typeof(jl_tparam0(b))) &&
+             jl_subtype(a, b);
+    }
+    if (jl_is_typevar(a)) {
+        jl_value_t *na = ((jl_tvar_t*)a)->ub;
+        hasfree &= jl_has_free_typevars(na);
+        return simple_subtype(na, b, hasfree);","path":"src/jltypes.c","commit_id":"443e1351b097e811c7e6a714ab5a5a244a9e8829","original_commit_id":"afee770a4f8e1e28674d785adcc7b4749d5c651f","user":{"login":"N5N3","id":22023842,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMDIzODQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22023842?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3","html_url":"https://github.com/N5N3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Well it turns out this branch might affect method definition.
On master
```julia
julia> f(x::Union{AbstractArray,T}) where {T<:Vector} = T
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> f([1])
ERROR: UndefVarError: `T` not defined
Stacktrace:
 [1] f(x::Vector{Int64})
   @ Main .\REPL[3]:1
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[4]:1
```
This PR
```julia
julia> f(x::Union{AbstractArray,T}) where {T<:Vector} = T
WARNING: method definition for f at REPL[1]:1 declares type variable T but does not use it.
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> f([1])
ERROR: UndefVarError: `T` not defined in static parameter matching
Suggestion: run Test.detect_unbound_args to detect method arguments that do not fully constrain a type parameter.
Stacktrace:
 [1] f(x::Vector{Int64})
   @ Main .\REPL[1]:1
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:1
```
Not sure if this is a :2,"created_at":"2019-09-02T23:04:16Z","updated_at":"2024-01-26T02:37:40Z","closed_at":"2024-01-26T02:37:40Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"In Julia 1.2.0,
```julia
julia> (Tuple{Q,Int} where Q<:Int) <: Tuple{T,T} where T
false

julia> (Tuple{Int,Int}) <: Tuple{T,T} where T
true
```
I think the first check should return `true` because the types on the left-hand side are equivalent:
```julia
julia> (Tuple{Q,Int} where Q<:Int) <: Tuple{Int,Int}
true

julia> Tuple{Int,Int} <: Tuple{Q,Int} where Q<:Int
true
```

throwing"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-01-23T14:10:41Z","updated_at":"2024-01-26T02:37:41Z","closed_at":"2024-01-26T02:37:41Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Here's a Git repository containing a Julia package that generates counterexamples to the transitivity property that `<:` is supposed to satisfy:

https://gitlab.com/nsajko/SubtypingRelationExperiments.jl

So far I tested it on v1.10 and v1.11-DEV: the results between these two versions are the same and may be found under `results/`. I'll give just two of the counterexamples here, see the rest on my repo:

This is the first counterexample in the results:

```
counterexample triplet:  (Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}, Tuple{C, C}, Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C)
```

In the REPL:

```julia-repl
julia> struct C end

julia> const T1 = Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}
Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}

julia> const T2 = Tuple{C, C}
Tuple{C, C}

julia> const T3 = Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C
Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C

julia> T1 <: T2 <: T3
true

julia> T1 <: T3
false
```

This is the last counterexample in the results:

```
counterexample triplet:  (Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C, Tuple{Any, Vararg{C}}, Tuple{Any, Vararg{X}} where X>:C)
```

In the REPL:

```julia-repl
julia> abstract type A end

julia> struct C <: A end

julia> const T1 = Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C
Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C

throwing"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-01-23T14:10:41Z","updated_at":"2024-01-26T02:37:41Z","closed_at":"2024-01-26T02:37:41Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Here's a Git repository containing a Julia package that generates counterexamples to the transitivity property that `<:` is supposed to satisfy:

https://gitlab.com/nsajko/SubtypingRelationExperiments.jl

So far I tested it on v1.10 and v1.11-DEV: the results between these two versions are the same and may be found under `results/`. I'll give just two of the counterexamples here, see the rest on my repo:

This is the first counterexample in the results:

```
counterexample triplet:  (Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}, Tuple{C, C}, Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C)
```

In the REPL:

```julia-repl
julia> struct C end

julia> const T1 = Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}
Tuple{X, X} where X<:Union{}

julia> const T2 = Tuple{C, C}
Tuple{C, C}

julia> const T3 = Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C
Tuple{X, X, Vararg{Any}} where C<:X<:C

julia> T1 <: T2 <: T3
true

julia> T1 <: T3
false
```

This is the last counterexample in the results:

```
counterexample triplet:  (Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C, Tuple{Any, Vararg{C}}, Tuple{Any, Vararg{X}} where X>:C)
```

In the REPL:

```julia-repl
julia> abstract type A end

julia> struct C <: A end

julia> const T1 = Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C
Tuple{A, X, Vararg{X}} where X<:C

julia> const T2 = Tuple{Any, Vararg{C}}
Tuple{Any, Vararg{C}}

julia> const T3 = Tuple{Any, Vararg{X}} where X>:C
Tuple{Any, Vararg{X}} where X>:C

julia> T1 <: T2 <: T3
true

julia> T1 <: T3
false
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53021/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53021/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

Yes, commutativity doesn't quite make sense considering over-estimation.
At present the algorithm would widen the result to the 2nd input (usually the method signature) if it thinks the union complexity is too big.
But for simple input, we usually give the smallest result thus keeps commutativity.

seems like a regression: in Julia v1.9-rc2 and master
```julia
julia> using LinearAlgebra, BenchmarkTools

julia> A = rand(ComplexF64,4,4,1000,1000);

julia> B = similar(A);

julia> a,b = (view(B,:,:,1,1),view(A,:,:,1,1));

julia> @btime mul!($b,$a,$a);
  321.792 ns (10 allocations: 608 bytes)
```
This is on an M1 Max
```julia
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.9.0-rc2
Commit 72aec423c2a (2023-04-01 10:41 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: macOS (arm64-apple-darwin21.3.0)
  CPU: 10 × Apple M1 Max
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-14.0.6 (ORCJIT, apple-m1)
  Threads: 1 on 8 virtual cores
Environment:
  JULIA_EDITOR = code
```

But in v1.8.5 we don't allocate
```julia
julia> using LinearAlgebra, BenchmarkTools

julia> A = rand(ComplexF64,4,4,1000,1000);

julia> B = similar(A);

julia> a,b = (view(B,:,:,1,1),view(A,:,:,1,1));

julia> @btime mul!($b,$a,$a);
  97.076 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
Note that if A has dimensions 3 or less, this issue does not arise.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49332/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49332/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

Before `isequal` is used for comparison, `hash` is used to find the slot.
Thus, equal values should have equal hashes, so as to meet at the same slot.

However, for UnionAll types, `hash` is unstable: equal values have different hashes:
```
julia> a=Array{T} where T
Array
julia> b=Array{T2} where T2
Array
julia> a==b, a===b, hash(a), hash(b)
(true, false, 0x3e735c1c362de00f, 0x6ba6f097cee26fae)
julia> unique([a,b])
2-element Vector{UnionAll}:
 Array
 Array
```

However, the error message is confusingly incorrect:
```julia
julia> x = [1]';

julia> x isa Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T
true

julia> x * x
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching *(::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}}, ::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}})

Closest candidates are:
  *(::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T, ::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/adjtrans.jl:481
  *(::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T, ::AbstractMatrix)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/matmul.jl:87
  *(::AbstractMatrix, ::AbstractMatrix, ::Number, ::Number)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/matmul.jl:1084
  ...

Stacktrace:
 [1] *(u::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}}, v::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/adjtrans.jl:481
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:1
```
Note that the first suggested method is the one that we're hitting, except this is throwing a `MethodError` again, which makes it seem like there is no match. We may potentially change this from a `MethodError` to a different type (perhaps `ArgumentError`), but perhaps this method is not necessary anyway? After this PR, we obtain
```julia
julia> x * x
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching *(::Vector{Int64}, ::Vector{Int64})

Closest candidates are:
  *(::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Any...)
   @ Base operators.jl:595
  *(::Dates.Period, ::AbstractArray)
   @ Dates ~/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/Dates/src/periods.jl:93
  *(::Number, ::AbstractArray)
   @ Base arraymath.jl:21
  ...

Stacktrace:
 [1] *(x::Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}}, A::Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/matmul.jl:87
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:1
```
This makes it clear that the real issue is that we can't multiply two vectors.","created_at":"2023-12-05T10:14:23Z","updated_at":"2023-12-05T14:54:22Z","closed_at":"2023-12-05T14:54:22Z","merged_at":"2023-12-05T14:54:22Z","merge_commit_sha":"28b252a30f0cf4c6469dc67bd0262fd740032a4b","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":61807486,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MTgwNzQ4Ng==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/linear%20algebra","name":"linear algebra","color":"f2b3db","default":false,"description":"Linear algebra"},{"id":1593898115,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNTkzODk4MTE1","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/merge%20me","name":"merge me","color":"00bb00","default":false,"description":"PR is reviewed. {"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":16134494450,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"28b252a30f0cf4c6469dc67bd0262fd740032a4b","before":"9c6fec13ed3a106a4c1e30d07d55eaa3701b5a59","commits":[{"sha":"28b252a30f0cf4c6469dc67bd0262fd740032a4b","author":{"email":"9724b4e6cd549fe9b1c21996f3f0108658b44517@gmail.com","name":"Jishnu Bhattacharya"},"message":"Remove confusing `::AdjointAbsVec * ::AdjointAbsVec` methods (#52401)

These methods presumably exist to throw an error at the top-level,
rather than after the arrays have been unwrapped. However, the error
message is confusingly incorrect:
```julia
julia> x = [1]';

julia> x isa Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T
true

julia> x * x
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching *(::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}}, ::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}})

Closest candidates are:
  *(::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T, ::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/adjtrans.jl:481
  *(::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{T, <:AbstractVector} where T, ::AbstractMatrix)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/matmul.jl:87
  *(::AbstractMatrix, ::AbstractMatrix, ::Number, ::Number)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/matmul.jl:1084
  ...

Stacktrace:
 [1] *(u::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}}, v::LinearAlgebra.Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/packages/julias/julia-latest/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/adjtrans.jl:481
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:1
```
Note that the first suggested method is the one that we're hitting,
except this is throwing a `MethodError` again, which makes it seem like
there is no match. We may potentially change this from a `MethodError`
to a different type (perhaps `ArgumentError`), but perhaps this method
is not necessary anyway? After this PR, we obtain
```julia
julia> x * x
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching *(::Vector{Int64}, ::Vector{Int64})

Closest candidates are:
  *(::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Any...)
   @ Base operators.jl:595
  *(::Dates.Period, ::AbstractArray)
   @ Dates ~/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/Dates/src/periods.jl:93
  *(::Number, ::AbstractArray)
   @ Base arraymath.jl:21
  ...

Stacktrace:
 [1] *(x::Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}}, A::Adjoint{Int64, Vector{Int64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/LinearAlgebra/src/matmul.jl:87
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:1
```
This makes it clear that the real issue is that we can't multiply two
vectors.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/28b252a30f0cf4c6469dc67bd0262fd740032a4b"}]}

{"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":16134500086,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"d0e7ec09bb77279b9eb56683197ead5fbe058bc5","before":"28b252a30f0cf4c6469dc67bd0262fd740032a4b","commits":[{"sha":"d0e7ec09bb77279b9eb56683197ead5fbe058bc5","author":{"email":"9724b4e6cd549fe9b1c21996f3f0108658b44517@gmail.com","name":"Jishnu Bhattacharya"},"message":"Adjoint for vectors may use trailing axes of the parent (#52379)

Close #52373, or at least the part that may be addressed here. After
this, the first axis for an `Adjoint(parent::AbstractVector)` will be
the second axis of the `parent`. This will change the type of the axis
where the parent array type specializes `axes(A, d)`. In the short term,
this would improve type-stability in cases such as
```julia
julia> A = OffsetArray([1,2], 2);

julia> @code_typed axes(A')[1]
CodeInfo(
1 ─ %1 = $(Expr(:boundscheck))::Bool
│   %2 = Base.getfield(t, i, %1)::OffsetArrays.IdOffsetRange{Int64, Base.OneTo{Int64}}
└──      return %2
) => OffsetArrays.IdOffsetRange{Int64, Base.OneTo{Int64}}
```
where the result is now concretely inferred instead of being a small
`Union`.
In principle, with
https://github.com/JuliaArrays/StaticArrays.jl/pull/916, this would make
the axes of the adjoint of a `StaticVector` statically sized.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/d0e7ec09bb77279b9eb56683197ead5fbe058bc5"}]}

PR fixes the bug caused by the trimming trick.
`Base.index_lengths` is not a proper tool to calculate the trimmed shape as `indices` might consume more than 1 dim.
And we can avoid the unneeded "`repeat`" when we meet `Array{CartesianIndex{0}}`

Test added.

present, out typeintersection "prefers" `Tuple` with more parameters.
This PR tries to replace `Tuple{Vararg{T,N}}` with `Tuple{T,T,T,Vararg{T,N}}` during re-intersection if we can prove that `N >= 3` and `N` is used only for Vararg length.
It is somewhat similar to #39098 but only fucus on the expansion.

Resolve some intersection regression and this TODO from #46446.

{"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":16279052502,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"d69bb97d960fb8c140f47766ed96e34117765653","before":"914743713b133ef1b4462f11799652bcbae28487","commits":[{"sha":"d69bb97d960fb8c140f47766ed96e34117765653","author":{"email":"329e29da7eb82e411422ea138c8f325aa7ff6190@qq.com","name":"N5N3"},"message":"Expand more Vararg elements during re-intersection if valid. (#46604)

Our type intersection "prefers" `Tuple` with more parameters.
This PR tries to replace `Tuple{Vararg{T,N}}` with
`Tuple{T,T,T,Vararg{T,N}}` during re-intersection if we can prove that
`N >= 3` and `N` is used only for Vararg length.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/d69bb97d960fb8c140f47766ed96e34117765653"}]}

{"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":16279068262,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"5195da2ca006d3e63dcd8973c7d867c88381f6be","before":"d69bb97d960fb8c140f47766ed96e34117765653","commits":[{"sha":"5195da2ca006d3e63dcd8973c7d867c88381f6be","author":{"email":"9724b4e6cd549fe9b1c21996f3f0108658b44517@gmail.com","name":"Jishnu Bhattacharya"},"message":"Improve linear indexing performance for FastSubArrays (#45371)

This PR forwards `AbstractUnitRange` indices for `FastSubArrays` to the
parent, making use of the fact that the parent might have efficient
vector indexing methods defined.

---------

```julia
julia> S = StructArray{Complex{Int}}(([1,2], [3,4]))
2-element StructArray(::Vector{Int64}, ::Vector{Int64}) with eltype Complex{Int64}:
 1 + 3im
 2 + 4im

julia> empty(S)
0-element StructArray(::Vector{Int64}, ::Vector{Int64}) with eltype Complex{Int64}
```
instead of
```julia
julia> empty(S)
Complex{Int64}[]
```
This behavior matches the docstring now: "Create an empty vector similar to `v`". Often, `similar` will fall back to creating a `Vector`, so the current behavior will be preserved.

inference"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-10-29T12:38:41Z","updated_at":"2023-12-18T12:55:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MWE:

```julia-repl
julia> map(l -> NTuple{l,Any}, 0:0)  # succeeds, as expected
1-element Vector{DataType}:
 Tuple{}

julia> const NT = NTuple{n, Any} where {n}
NTuple{n, Any} where n

julia> map(l -> NT{l}, 0:0)
ERROR: UndefVarError: `T` not defined
Stacktrace:
 [1] promote_typejoin_union(::Type{Union{Type{<:NTuple{n, Any}}, Type}})
   @ Base ./promotion.jl:185
 [2] _collect(c::UnitRange{Int64}, itr::Base.Generator{UnitRange{Int64}, var"#3#4"}, ::Base.EltypeUnknown, isz::Base.HasShape{1})
   @ Base ./array.jl:748
 [3] collect_similar(cont::UnitRange{Int64}, itr::Base.Generator{UnitRange{Int64}, var"#3#4"})
   @ Base ./array.jl:700
 [4] map(f::Function, A::UnitRange{Int64})
   @ Base ./abstractarray.jl:3305
```

Interestingly, the same code also suceeds in some other situations. In a [package](https://gitlab.com/nsajko/OptimalSortingNetworks.jl) I have this code:

```julia
const NT = NTuple{n,Any} where {n}
to_nt_type(lengths) = Union{map((n -> NT{n}), lengths)...}
const other_optimal_lengths = (10,)
const OtherOptimalLengths = to_nt_type(other_optimal_lengths)
```

`to_nt_type` fails when called directly, but the precompilation succeeds and `OtherOptimalLengths` is defined.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51927/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51927/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1860427338","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51927#issuecomment-1860427338","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51927","id":1860427338,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5u495K","user":{"login":"N5N3","id":22023842,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMDIzODQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22023842?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3","html_url":"https://github.com/N5N3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-12-18T12:55:31Z","updated_at":"2023-12-18T12:55:31Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"MWE
```julia
julia> const CC = Core.Compiler
Core.Compiler

julia> lattice = CC.typeinf_lattice(CC.NativeInterpreter())
Core.Compiler.InferenceLattice{Core.Compiler.ConditionalsLattice{Core.Compiler.PartialsLattice{Core.Compiler.ConstsLattice}}}(Core.Compiler.ConditionalsLattice{Core.Compiler.PartialsLattice{Core.Compiler.ConstsLattice}}(Core.Compiler.PartialsLattice{Core.Compiler.ConstsLattice}(Core.Compiler.ConstsLattice())))

julia> CC.apply_type_tfunc(lattice, CC.Const(Tuple{Vararg{Any,N}} where N), Int)
Type{<:NTuple{N, Any}}

julia> CC.apply_type_tfunc(lattice, CC.Const(Tuple{Vararg{Any,N}} where N), Int) |> Base.has_free_typevars
true
```
Apparently, the `istuple = isa(headtype, Type) && (headtype == Tuple)` check get fooled by
```julia
julia> Tuple{Vararg{Any,N}} where {N} == Tuple
true
```
Not sure why v1.9 is OK, but we should use `istuple = headtype === Tuple` or `istuple = isa(headtype, Type) && (headtype == Tuple) && !isa(headtype, UnionAll)`","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1860427338/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

behavior","user":{"login":"N5N3","id":22023842,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMDIzODQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22023842?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3","html_url":"https://github.com/N5N3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"1. If we use `BitArray`/`Array{Bool}` to index, `to_indices` has an optimiztion for linear-iteratable case. 
But the corresponding check is not correct. IIUC, this optimization is legal only when the Boolen array is the only index provided. 
The first commit fix it and widen this optimization to all Boolen array.
Before this PR
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 2×3×4 Array{Float64, 3} at index [1, 3×4 Matrix{Bool}]
```
After
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
true
```

2. On master, if the index/array has singleton trailing dimension, boundcheck of logical index show different behavior depending on the number of indexes provided.
If there's only one index variable, singleton dimension wil not be ignored. The second commit fix it. (close behavior","user":{"login":"N5N3","id":22023842,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMDIzODQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22023842?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3","html_url":"https://github.com/N5N3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"1. If we use `BitArray`/`Array{Bool}` to index, `to_indices` has an optimiztion for linear-iteratable case. 
But the corresponding check is not correct. IIUC, this optimization is legal only when the Boolen array is the only index provided. 
The first commit fix it and widen this optimization to all Boolen array.
Before this PR
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 2×3×4 Array{Float64, 3} at index [1, 3×4 Matrix{Bool}]
```
After
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
true
```

2. On master, if the index/array has singleton trailing dimension, boundcheck of logical index show different behavior depending on the number of indexes provided.
If there's only one index variable, singleton dimension wil not be ignored. The second commit fix it. (close behavior","user":{"login":"N5N3","id":22023842,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMDIzODQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22023842?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3","html_url":"https://github.com/N5N3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"1. If we use `BitArray`/`Array{Bool}` to index, `to_indices` has an optimiztion for linear-iteratable case. 
But the corresponding check is not correct. IIUC, this optimization is legal only when the Boolen array is the only index provided. 
The first commit fix it and widen this optimization to all Boolen array.
Before this PR
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 2×3×4 Array{Float64, 3} at index [1, 3×4 Matrix{Bool}]
```
After
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
true
```

2. On master, if the index/array has singleton trailing dimension, boundcheck of logical index show different behavior depending on the number of indexes provided.
If there's only one index variable, singleton dimension wil not be ignored. The second commit fix it. (close behavior","user":{"login":"N5N3","id":22023842,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMDIzODQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22023842?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3","html_url":"https://github.com/N5N3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"1. If we use `BitArray`/`Array{Bool}` to index, `to_indices` has an optimiztion for linear-iteratable case. 
But the corresponding check is not correct. IIUC, this optimization is legal only when the Boolen array is the only index provided. 
The first commit fix it and widen this optimization to all Boolen array.
Before this PR
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 2×3×4 Array{Float64, 3} at index [1, 3×4 Matrix{Bool}]
```
After
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
true
```

2. On master, if the index/array has singleton trailing dimension, boundcheck of logical index show different behavior depending on the number of indexes provided.
If there's only one index variable, singleton dimension wil not be ignored. The second commit fix it. (close in https://buildkite.com/julialang/julia-master/builds/31309#018c7f0b-aada-4017-bb2f-44001593ac6b.
On master keyword call might taint `inaccessiblememonly`.
MWE: 
```julia
julia> foo(; kws...) = 1
foo (generic function with 1 method)

julia> Base.infer_effects(foo, Tuple{})
(+c,+e,+n,+t,+s,!m,+u)
```
which is caused by
```julia
julia> Base.infer_effects(()->Vararg)
(+c,+e,+n,+t,+s,!m,+u)
```

in https://buildkite.com/julialang/julia-master/builds/31309#018c7f0b-aada-4017-bb2f-44001593ac6b.
On master keyword call might taint `inaccessiblememonly`.
MWE: 
```julia
julia> foo(; kws...) = 1
foo (generic function with 1 method)

julia> Base.infer_effects(foo, Tuple{})
(+c,+e,+n,+t,+s,!m,+u)
```
which is caused by
```julia
julia> Base.infer_effects(()->Vararg)
(+c,+e,+n,+t,+s,!m,+u)
```

inference"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-10-29T12:38:41Z","updated_at":"2023-12-20T14:17:34Z","closed_at":"2023-12-20T14:17:34Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MWE:

```julia-repl
julia> map(l -> NTuple{l,Any}, 0:0)  # succeeds, as expected
1-element Vector{DataType}:
 Tuple{}

NTuple{n, Any} where n

ERROR: UndefVarError: `T` not defined
Stacktrace:
 [1] promote_typejoin_union(::Type{Union{Type{<:NTuple{n, Any}}, Type}})
   @ Base ./promotion.jl:185
 [2] _collect(c::UnitRange{Int64}, itr::Base.Generator{UnitRange{Int64}, var"#3#4"}, ::Base.EltypeUnknown, isz::Base.HasShape{1})
   @ Base ./array.jl:748

   @ Base ./array.jl:700
 [4] map(f::Function, A::UnitRange{Int64})
   @ Base ./abstractarray.jl:3305
```

Interestingly, the same code also suceeds in some other situations. In a [package](https://gitlab.com/nsajko/OptimalSortingNetworks.jl) I have this code:

```julia
const NT = NTuple{n,Any} where {n}
to_nt_type(lengths) = Union{map((n -> NT{n}), lengths)...}
const other_optimal_lengths = (10,)
const OtherOptimalLengths = to_nt_type(other_optimal_lengths)
```

`to_nt_type` fails when called directly, but the precompilation succeeds and `OtherOptimalLengths` is defined.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51927/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51927/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

Output

```julia ERROR: ArgumentError: reducing with add_sum over an empty collection of element type Union{} is not allowed. You may be able to prevent this error by supplying an `init` value to the reducer. Stacktrace: [1] _empty_reduce_error(f::Any, T::Type) @ Base ./reduce.jl:320 [2] reduce_empty(::typeof(Base.add_sum), ::Core.TypeofBottom) @ Base ./reduce.jl:351 [3] reduce_empty(op::Base.BottomRF{typeof(Base.add_sum)}, ::Type{Union{}}) @ Base ./reduce.jl:360 [4] reduce_empty_iter @ ./reduce.jl:384 [inlined] [5] reduce_empty_iter @ ./reduce.jl:383 [inlined] [6] foldl_impl(op::Base.BottomRF{typeof(Base.add_sum)}, nt::Base._InitialValue, itr::Tuple{}) @ Base ./reduce.jl:49 [7] mapfoldl_impl(f::typeof(identity), op::typeof(Base.add_sum), nt::Base._InitialValue, itr::Tuple{}) @ Base ./reduce.jl:44 [8] mapfoldl(f::Function, op::Function, itr::Tuple{}; init::Base._InitialValue) @ Base ./reduce.jl:175 [9] mapfoldl @ Base ./reduce.jl:175 [inlined] [10] mapreduce @ Base ./reduce.jl:307 [inlined] [11] sum(f::Function, a::Tuple{}) @ Base ./reduce.jl:535 [12] sum(a::Tuple{}; kw::@Kwargs{}) @ Base ./reduce.jl:564 [13] macro expansion @ ./multidimensional.jl:1625 [inlined] [14] permutedims!(P::Array{Int64, 0}, B::Array{Int64, 0}, perm::Tuple{}) @ Base ./multidimensional.jl:1615 [15] permutedims(B::Array{Int64, 0}, perm::Tuple{}) @ Base ./multidimensional.jl:1600 [16] top-level scope @ REPL[1]:1 ```

{"action":"closed","number":45869,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/45869","id":983355442,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM46nNAy","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/45869","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/45869.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/45869.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45869","number":45869,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Fix inconsistant logical index behavior","user":{"login":"N5N3","id":22023842,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMDIzODQy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22023842?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3","html_url":"https://github.com/N5N3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/N5N3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"1. If we use `BitArray`/`Array{Bool}` to index, `to_indices` has an optimiztion for linear-iteratable case. 
But the corresponding check is not correct. IIUC, this optimization is legal only when the Boolen array is the only index provided. 
The first commit fix it and widen this optimization to all Boolen array.
Before this PR
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 2×3×4 Array{Float64, 3} at index [1, 3×4 Matrix{Bool}]
```
After
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
true
```

2. On master, if the index/array has singleton trailing dimension, boundcheck of logical index show different behavior depending on the number of indexes provided.
If there's only one index variable, singleton dimension wil not be ignored. The second commit fix it. (close {"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":16402360955,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"933a83af6af1a5d272095a8875192f944b182f29","before":"4e4c0e5eda31c5d85f8500ad621b89f77e9d4a90","commits":[{"sha":"933a83af6af1a5d272095a8875192f944b182f29","author":{"email":"329e29da7eb82e411422ea138c8f325aa7ff6190@qq.com","name":"N5N3"},"message":"Fix inconsistant logical index behavior (#45869)

1. If we use `BitArray`/`Array{Bool}` to index, `to_indices` has an
optimiztion for linear-iteratable case.
But the corresponding check is not correct. IIUC, this optimization is
legal only when the Boolen array is the only index provided.
The first commit fix it and widen this optimization to all Boolen array.
Before this PR
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 2×3×4 Array{Float64, 3} at index [1, 3×4 Matrix{Bool}]
```
After
```julia
julia> A = rand(2,3,4); I = rand(Bool,3,4);

julia> A[1,I] == A[1,view(I,:,:)]
true
```

2. On master, if the index/array has singleton trailing dimension,
boundcheck of logical index show different behavior depending on the
number of indexes provided.
If there's only one index variable, singleton dimension wil not be
ignored. The second commit fix it. (close #45867)

---------

Co-authored-by: Matt Bauman <mbauman@gmail.com>","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/933a83af6af1a5d272095a8875192f944b182f29"}]}

s]"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-06-29T23:43:05Z","updated_at":"2023-12-25T12:54:49Z","closed_at":"2023-12-25T12:54:49Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The documentation for [logical indexing](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/manual/arrays/#Logical-indexing) says that indexing by a N-dimensional boolean array is effectively the same as indexing by the vector of `CartesianIndex{N}`s where its values are `true`.

However, this does not appear to behave correctly in Julia’s current implementation.

In the simple example below, indexing `array` with a vector of `CartesianIndex{2}` succeeds, while indexing it with the equivalent two-dimensional logical index fails:

```jl
julia> array = [1];

julia> index = [true;;];

julia> findall(index)
1-element Vector{CartesianIndex{2}}:
 CartesianIndex(1, 1)

julia> array[findall(index)] # correct
1-element Vector{Int64}:
 1

julia> array[index] # incorrect?
ERROR: BoundsError: attempt to access 1-element Vector{Int64} at index [1×1 Matrix{Bool}]
Stacktrace:
 [1] throw_boundserror(A::Vector{Int64}, I::Tuple{Base.LogicalIndex{Int64, Matrix{Bool}}})
   @ Base ./abstractarray.jl:691
 [2] checkbounds
   @ ./abstractarray.jl:656 [inlined]
 [3] _getindex
   @ ./multidimensional.jl:838 [inlined]
 [4] getindex(A::Vector{Int64}, I::Matrix{Bool})
   @ Base ./abstractarray.jl:1218
 [5] top-level scope
   @ REPL[5]:1
```

**Update:** The docs additionally say that

> A logical index must be a vector of the same length as the dimension it indexes into, or it must be the only index provided and match the size and dimensionality of the array it indexes into.

In the above case, neither condition applies, so it seems that this is a documented edge case. 

It might also be the explanation for a confusing behavior [encountered](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/slicing-and-boolean-indexing-in-multidimensional-arrays/83522) earlier by a Julia user, but I don't understand well enough to say.


This test was run on Julia 1.7.3.

```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.7.3
Commit 742b9abb4d (2022-05-06 12:58 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: macOS (x86_64-apple-darwin21.4.0)
  CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9980HK CPU @ 2.40GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm

{"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":16402369502,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/master","head":"713560bb4fe190d59b212021a5d0eda5ffba1010","before":"933a83af6af1a5d272095a8875192f944b182f29","commits":[{"sha":"713560bb4fe190d59b212021a5d0eda5ffba1010","author":{"email":"9724b4e6cd549fe9b1c21996f3f0108658b44517@gmail.com","name":"Jishnu Bhattacharya"},"message":"Specialize axes for structured matrices (#52480)

On master
```julia
julia> S = SMatrix{4,4}(1:16);

julia> A = StructArray{Complex{Int}}((S,S));

julia> axes(Symmetric(A))
(Base.OneTo(4), Base.OneTo(4))
```
The static axes are lost. After this,
```julia
julia> axes(Symmetric(A))
(SOneTo(4), SOneTo(4))
```
Similarly, I've added methods for other structured matrix types. These
help with other cases like infinite arrays.

---------

PR make `StridedReinterpretArray`'s `get/setindex` pure pointer based if its root parent is a `Array`.
Thus a "Dense" `ReinterpretArray` should behave more like a `Array`.
Some examples with better performance:
```julia
julia> a = randn(ComplexF64, 100, 100); b = randn(100); c = a * b;
julia> aa = reinterpret(Float64, a); cc = reinterpret(Float64, c);
julia> @btime LinearAlgebra.generic_matvecmul!($cc, 'N', $aa, $b, LinearAlgebra.MulAddMul(true,false));
  2.544 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)  # on master 116.900 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
```julia
julia> f(x, y) = @inbounds @simd ivdep for i in eachindex(x,y) # ivdep here is useless on master
       x[i] = ntoh(y[i])
       end

julia> a = reinterpret(Float64, rand(UInt8, 16000));

julia> @btime f($a, $a)
  124.388 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes) # on master 697.260 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

julia> @btime LinearAlgebra.generic_matvecmul!($cc, 'N', $aa, $b, LinearAlgebra.MulAddMul(true,false));
  2.544 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)  # on master 116.900 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

       end

julia> a = reinterpret(Float64, rand(UInt8, 16000));

  124.388 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes) # on master 697.260 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
